opp.name: BP_S2P_Controltower Global
opp.next_step: pending travel Pending Dates from Sid (Lead) in the BP Digital Garage for Demo - travel issues, could be in India, dubai or UK.
opp.ais_why:
• Product scope is fully traditional S2P (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRequest, iRisk, iSave, iSource, iSupplier) with no AI modules (no Merlin, no ANA, no Agentic AI, no ML iAnalyze). The 8-module traditional-S2P breadth without any AI SKU is a strong signal that the deal is positioned for procurement-platform coverage rather than AI capability differentiation.
• Zero Avoma meetings on record despite BD_Engagement Engaged status and Formal Evaluation stage. LastActivityDate 2025-12-08 indicates ~5 months of staleness. The contradiction between Engaged BD + Formal Evaluation + zero meetings + 5-month staleness is unusual and may reflect that the engagement is happening via channels outside Avoma (e.g., partner-mediated or email-only).
• Medium confidence: Engaged BD is offset by complete absence of AI products in scope and missing meeting evidence. Defaulted DOWN at 3/4 boundary. Score could move UP if a fresh engagement surfaces AI product additions to the scope; would stay at 3 if the deal closes as a pure traditional S2P platform consolidation.

sources: Avoma: 1 touchpoint (8 Dec 2025 demo), not recorded, no content
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, pain, or ROI case is visible in any source. The opportunity Description, Business_Objectives__c, Customer_Business_Problem__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c fields are empty or not populated. The single logged touchpoint on 8 Dec 2025 (Procurement Control Tower Demo) was not recorded, so no buyer-stated pain, cost savings target, cycle-time reduction, or spend-under-management goal is available. The product scope (iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, iRisk) implies a full S2P+contract suite, but without a buyer conversation we do not know the operational gap they are solving, the financial impact, or the compelling event driving urgency. With 200 days of silence and zero stakeholder engagement, the value case has never been articulated or quantified. The immediate move is to re-engage, run discovery depth, and quantify the business problem with the buyer's finance and procurement leads.

sources: SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Champion_Identified__c: false or empty
SF OpportunityContactRole: 0 buyer-side entries, no Champion role
Avoma: 8 Dec 2025 demo attendees include Rebecca Macoustra, no advocacy evidence post-demo

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c and Champion_Identified__c are false or empty, and zero OpportunityContactRole entries carry a Champion role. The 8 Dec 2025 demo drew several BP attendees (Rebecca Macoustra, fee_min.chee@bp.com, quayum.miah@uk.bp.com, tusti.ghosh@bp.com, brittany.crosby@bp.com), and Rebecca Macoustra (Procurement Manager) appears on recent Salesforce task contacts, making her the closest candidate for champion development. However, with 200 days of radio silence and no logged follow-up engagement, there is no evidence she is advocating for Zycus internally, introducing us to senior stakeholders, sharing competitive intelligence, or driving the evaluation forward. The Next_Step__c reference to 'Sid (Lead) in the BP Digital Garage' suggests Sid may be a project sponsor or internal advocate for digital procurement innovation, but Sid has never been added as a contact role, no email or full name is on record, and the pending demo was never scheduled. Without an active, engaged champion with access to the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, this deal cannot progress. The ranked move is to re-engage Rebecca Macoustra and the demo attendees, assess their willingness and ability to champion Zycus internally, and if none are strong advocates, identify and cultivate a new champion with executive access and a vested interest in the S2P transformation.

sources: Avoma: 8 Dec 2025 demo (not recorded, no competitive discussion captured)
competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is named as the competitor in Salesforce Competitors__c, and given BP's scale and the S2P+contract scope, Ariba is a credible peer threat and likely the incumbent or a finalist. However, the competitive intelligence is thin: we have no buyer quote about Ariba's strengths or weaknesses, no pricing benchmark, no feature-gap analysis, and no insight into how BP views the Zycus vs Ariba trade-offs (e.g. configurability, AI/Merlin capabilities, integration complexity, cost). The 8 Dec 2025 demo was not recorded, so any competitive positioning or Ariba objections raised on that call are lost. With 200 days of silence, we do not know if Ariba has advanced, if BP has engaged other vendors (Coupa, Ivalua, GEP, Jaggaer), or if the evaluation has stalled entirely. The wedge to win against Ariba is typically Zycus's configurability, end-to-end S2P+contract integration, lower TCO, and AI-driven procurement intelligence (Merlin's spend analytics, contract risk detection, supplier performance prediction), but without a live buyer conversation we cannot validate or activate that narrative. The ranked move is to re-engage, run a competitive review with the buying committee, surface BP's Ariba concerns (cost, complexity, SAP lock-in, AI limitations), and position Zycus's Merlin-powered control-tower capabilities as the differentiated answer to their global procurement visibility and risk-management needs.

sources: Avoma: 8 Dec 2025 demo (not recorded, no pain statement captured)
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, and Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c are empty or not populated. The opportunity name 'BP_S2P_Controltower Global' suggests a desire for centralized procurement visibility and control across a global operation, and the eight-product scope (spanning sourcing, contracts, supplier management, procurement, invoicing, requisitions, and risk) implies BP is seeking to consolidate or modernize a fragmented procurement stack. However, we have no buyer quote confirming the pain, no stated cost of the current state (manual processes, maverick spend, supplier risk exposure, contract leakage), and no compelling event (regulatory pressure, M&A integration, cost-reduction mandate, digital transformation initiative). The 8 Dec 2025 demo attendee list included procurement specialists and a Digital Garage lead, hinting at a digital-innovation-driven initiative, but the call was not recorded so we have no verbatim pain statement. Without confirmed pain and a named pain owner (likely a VP Procurement or Chief Procurement Officer), the deal lacks urgency and a defensible business case. The immediate move is to re-engage, run discovery with the procurement and finance leads, surface and document the operational pain, quantify the cost, and identify the executive pain owner.

sources: Avoma: 8 Dec 2025 demo (not recorded, no commercial/contract discussion visible)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, and SI/partner involvement are all unknown. No MSA, SOW, or Order Form discussion is visible. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is empty, so we do not know if BP has an incumbent contract expiring that would create a forcing function. Given BP's size and the eight-product scope, a deal of this nature would typically require InfoSec review, ERP/SAP integration scoping, data residency and compliance checks, and potentially a multi-year MSA with phased rollout, but none of that is documented. The 8 Dec 2025 demo was an early-stage product walkthrough with no commercial or contracting conversation. With 200 days of silence and zero stakeholder engagement, the paper process has never been initiated. The move is to re-engage, confirm the deal is still active, and if so, map out the procurement mechanics (BP's vendor onboarding, legal review cycle, InfoSec questionnaire, and any SI/consulting partner they are using for implementation) before attempting to advance to commercials.

sources: Avoma: 8 Dec 2025 demo attendee list (no C-level or exec sponsor visible)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. Zero OpportunityContactRole entries exist on this opportunity, so no EB, Executive Sponsor, or CFO-level contact is mapped. The account-level contact list includes procurement specialists (Rebecca Macoustra, Jodie Beers, Julia Robinson), category leads (Roberto Straffi, Hardeep Singh, Jason Deans), and digital transformation roles (Antoni Bohdanowicz, Sam Townsend, Jamie Kaminski), but none are confirmed as involved in THIS opportunity's scope (BP_S2P_Controltower Global). The 8 Dec 2025 demo attendee list includes mid-level BP procurement and project contacts (fee_min.chee@bp.com, quayum.miah@uk.bp.com, rebecca.macoustra@uk.bp.com, tusti.ghosh@bp.com, brittany.crosby@bp.com), but no C-level or budget-authority role. With a $300k ACV and an enterprise S2P suite, the EB is likely a VP Procurement, CFO, or Chief Procurement Officer, but that person has never surfaced in any logged engagement. The deal cannot advance to commercials or close without mapping and engaging the budget holder. The ranked move is to re-engage the demo attendees, ask who controls the S2P budget and procurement platform decision, and secure an introduction.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process is unmapped. What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, and Multiple_approvals__c are empty or not read. The call sequence offers no clarity: the single logged touchpoint (8 Dec 2025 demo) brought mid-level procurement and project contacts but no senior decision-maker or approval authority. We do not know if BP is running a formal RFP (likely given the product scope and the Ariba competition), who the evaluation committee members are, what the shortlist timeline looks like, or what gates must pass before vendor selection. The Next_Step__c field references 'pending dates from Sid (Lead) in the BP Digital Garage for Demo' with travel logistics uncertainty, but no follow-up demo was ever scheduled or logged, and the thread has been silent for 200 days. With no stakeholder map and no process visibility, we cannot forecast when or how a decision will happen. The ranked move is to re-engage the demo attendees (especially Rebecca Macoustra, who appears on tasks, and the Digital Garage lead Sid if reachable), reconstruct the evaluation timeline, map the approval chain, and confirm whether this opportunity is still live or has stalled/been shelved.

sources: SF Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c: empty or not populated
Avoma: 8 Dec 2025 demo (not recorded, no feedback captured)
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's evaluation criteria are unknown. Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, and Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c are empty or not populated. The 8 Dec 2025 demo was titled 'Procurement Control Tower Demo', suggesting a focus on spend visibility, supplier risk, or procurement analytics, but the call was not recorded so we have no buyer feedback on fit, no test-case validation, and no stated decision criteria. The eight-product scope (iContract through iRisk, spanning S2P and contract lifecycle) implies a comprehensive RFP or multi-module evaluation, but we do not know the must-have vs nice-to-have features, the technical integration requirements (ERP, e-procurement, supplier portals), the user adoption/change-management bar, or how AI/Merlin capabilities factor into scoring. SAP Ariba is named as the competitor, which suggests BP is running a bake-off, but without the RFP document, scorecard, or shortlist feedback we are flying blind. The next move is to re-engage, request the formal RFP or evaluation scorecard if one exists, and run a fit workshop to surface and document the decision criteria with the buying committee.

The opportunity scope (Procurement Control Tower, eight-product S2P+contract suite including iRisk) suggests BP is seeking AI-driven procurement intelligence and supplier risk management, which aligns with Zycus's Merlin capabilities. The deal is cold and off track.

items.detail: No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified or engaged; zero senior-level stakeholder mapping for a $300k enterprise deal. Without executive alignment and budget authority, the deal cannot advance to commercials or close.

Deal has been dark for 200 days (6.5 months) with zero buyer engagement since 8 Dec 2025 demo. Close date 31 Oct 2026 is 127 days out, but with no active buying process, no mapped stakeholders, and no forward movement, the October close is indefensible and the deal is at high risk of indefinite stall or lost status.

Zero champion identified or developed; closest candidate (Rebecca Macoustra) has not engaged in 200 days. Without an internal advocate with executive access and a vested interest in driving the evaluation, Zycus has no internal champion to navigate the buying committee, counter SAP Ariba, or push the decision forward.

Single demo on 8 Dec 2025 was not recorded; all buyer feedback, competitive positioning, fit gaps, and demo content are lost. This eliminates the primary source of early-stage intelligence and makes it impossible to validate whether the demo resonated or where objections lie.
Zero OpportunityContactRole buyer-side entries; deal is unthreaded with no documented stakeholder map, making it impossible to multi-thread, identify power roles, or navigate the buying committee.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 31 Oct 2025, entered Formal Evaluation by 8 Dec 2025 (39 days). Has remained in Formal Evaluation for 200 days since last activity. Close date 31 Oct 2026 is 127 days out. A deal dark for 200 days (6.5+ months) with zero stakeholder mapping, no identified EB/DM/Champion, and no buyer response is forced Off Track. At current zero-pace, the remaining discovery, shortlisting, commercials, negotiation, and contracting gates cannot complete by October. Stage velocity: 200+ days in Formal Evaluation with no progression signal is a full stall.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal has been completely silent for 200 days with zero buyer engagement since December 2025 demo. No champion, economic buyer, or stakeholder mapping exists. SAP Ariba incumbent and no relationship means no path to close in 127 days.

best_practice_check.summary: This $300k enterprise S2P deal has been dark for 200 days (6.5 months) with zero buyer engagement, no mapped stakeholders, and no identified Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion. The single demo on 8 Dec 2025 was not recorded, so all buyer feedback is lost. With 127 days to close and the deal stuck in Formal Evaluation, the only path forward is aggressive re-engagement and qualification to determine if the opportunity is genuinely live or should be disqualified. If live, the immediate priorities are to map the buying committee, identify and engage the Economic Buyer, develop a champion with executive access, and run a fit workshop to document requirements and competitive positioning against SAP Ariba.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the sole named competitor and is a credible high threat given BP's enterprise scale and the S2P+contract scope. Ariba is likely the incumbent or a live finalist. No other vendors are named in any source. However, competitive intelligence is minimal: no buyer feedback on Ariba's strengths/gaps, no pricing data, and no recent engagement to validate current threat level. The 8 Dec 2025 demo was not recorded, so any Ariba objections or positioning from that call are lost. With 200 days of silence, we do not know if Ariba has advanced, if BP is evaluating other peers (Coupa, Ivalua, GEP), or if the entire procurement has stalled. Ariba remains the strongest current threat by default, but the stale intelligence and complete buyer disengagement make any competitive ranking speculative. The wedge to win is Zycus's configurability, lower TCO, AI/Merlin procurement intelligence (spend analytics, contract risk, supplier performance), and end-to-end S2P+contract integration vs Ariba's complexity and SAP lock-in, but this narrative has never been activated with the buyer.

However, the call was not recorded, so we have no buyer feedback on fit, gaps, or must-have features. With 200 days of silence and zero documented requirements, we cannot assess alignment or exposure on any dimension (technical integration, AI/Merlin appetite, user adoption, pricing, implementation timeline, ERP compatibility). The move is to re-engage and run a fit workshop.

• Product scope is fully traditional S2P (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRequest, iRisk, iSave, iSource, iSupplier) with no AI modules (no Merlin, no ANA, no Agentic AI, no ML iAnalyze). The 8-module traditional-S2P breadth without any AI SKU is a strong signal that the deal is positioned for procurement-platform coverage rather than AI capability differentiation.
• Zero Avoma meetings on record despite BD_Engagement Engaged status and Formal Evaluation stage. LastActivityDate 2025-12-08 indicates ~5 months of staleness. The contradiction between Engaged BD + Formal Evaluation + zero meetings + 5-month staleness is unusual and may reflect that the engagement is happening via channels outside Avoma (e.g., partner-mediated or email-only).
• Medium confidence: Engaged BD is offset by complete absence of AI products in scope and missing meeting evidence. Defaulted DOWN at 3/4 boundary. Score could move UP if a fresh engagement surfaces AI product additions to the scope; would stay at 3 if the deal closes as a pure traditional S2P platform consolidation.

deltas.subject: Deal is completely dark for 200 days (6.5 months) with zero buyer engagement since the 8 Dec 2025 demo; immediate priority is to re-engage the demo attendees (Rebecca Macoustra, fee_min.chee@bp.com, quayum.miah@uk.bp.com, tusti.ghosh@bp.com, brittany.crosby@bp.com) and the Digital Garage lead Sid referenced in Next_Step__c, determine if the opportunity is still active, and qualify or disqualify within 10 business days.

deltas.subject: Zero OpportunityContactRole buyer-side contacts on record; the deal is single-threaded at best or unthreaded in reality. Multi-threading is mission-critical: add the demo attendees (especially Rebecca Macoustra and Sid from BP Digital Garage) as contact roles immediately, map the procurement leadership (VP Procurement, Chief Procurement Officer), identify the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, and build a champion with access to budget authority and the ability to drive the evaluation internally.

deltas.subject: No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified or engaged for a $300k enterprise S2P suite; without a mapped budget holder and senior decision-maker, this deal cannot progress to commercials or close. The re-engagement move must prioritize mapping the EB (likely VP Procurement or CFO) and securing an introduction and validation meeting before any further product or commercial discussion.

deltas.subject: SAP Ariba is the named competitor but competitive intelligence is minimal (no buyer feedback, no pricing data, no gap analysis); if the deal re-engages, run a competitive review workshop within 14 days to surface BP's Ariba concerns (cost, complexity, SAP lock-in, AI limitations) and position Zycus's Merlin-powered procurement control tower (AI-driven spend analytics, contract risk, supplier performance) as the differentiated, agile, lower-TCO alternative.
